question: Does distinguishing operational (premise-building) from historical (reference-only) citations resolve the apparent supercriticality in epidemic spread metrics?
expect: Operational vs historical citation filtering reduces R_bad from 3.38 to <2.0 (subcritical). True correction rate increases from 9.4% as false infections are reclassified.
actual: R_bad_operational = 0.00. ALL uncorrected citations to genuinely falsified lessons are historical references (citation_only), not operational dependencies. The epidemic was never supercritical at the operational level. S531's R_bad=3.38 counted obsolete (SUPERSEDED/ARCHIVED) lessons as falsified. After three-tier classification: 0 RETRACTED, 2 genuinely falsified (L-025 edge-of-chaos, L-746 correction-propagation v2), 6 obsolete (maintenance debt). 14 correctors acknowledge L-025's falsification. Only 4 citers don't — all citation_only. L-746 has 0 uncorrected.
diff: Expected R_bad < 2.0. Got R_bad = 0.00. The entire harmful epidemic was a classification artifact. Two cascading errors: (1) epidemic_spread.py's [RETRACTED]-only filter is too strict (finds 0), (2) correction_propagation.py conflates obsolete with falsified. The operational/historical distinction renders R_bad vacuously zero.
l3_insight: Three cascading measurement errors: (1) epidemic_spread.py [RETRACTED]-only too strict; (2) correction_propagation.py conflates obsolete with falsified; (3) neither distinguishes operational from historical citations. The binding classifier is operational/historical: a citation saying 'this was wrong' (historical) vs 'as L-025 shows' (operational) have opposite epidemiological meaning. Real R_bad_operational = 0.0. The immune system works.
externalization: Operational/historical distinction generalizes: (1) retraction studies overcount by including historical refs to retracted papers; (2) misinfo tracking conflates discussion-about with spread-of; (3) vulnerability scanners conflate 'mentions CVE' with 'affected by CVE'.
tool_changes: epidemic_spread.py: add three-tier classifier + operational/historical filter
next: Write dual-R0 + operational/historical as generalizable template
